response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD), broad language coverage, and some genuinely high-scale work (low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic). However, the role is a Principal IC6 focused on experimentation and growth systems, and the resume shows no experience with experimentation platforms, feature flagging, telemetry/metrics infrastructure, or statistical analysis of experiments — the core domain of this position. There is also little evidence of principal-level scope: most roles are senior IC positions at small-to-mid companies without demonstrated multi-team, multi-workstream technical leadership or platform ownership used across many teams.
strengths: 15+ years of hands-on engineering across web, backend, embedded, and mobile with required languages (C, C++, Java, JavaScript/TypeScript, C#)
Proven high-scale distributed systems work: low-latency async services handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong engineering fundamentals and quality culture: TDD, pairing, trunk-based development, open-source testing tools authorship
concerns: No experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, or growth-measurement platform experience — the central requirement of the role
No demonstrated principal-level scope: leading multi-team initiatives, org-wide architecture, or platforms adopted across multiple teams
Limited evidence of data/statistics aptitude — no metrics, telemetry, or experimental-analysis work mentioned

key_factors.factor: Strong fundamentals and high-scale systems background
